rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by tracing and correcting the first invalid reference on the same line.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, validate that all referenced identifiers exist in the current AADL scope before use.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex to avoid reference resolution failures.
Ensure AGREE scalar type names match AADL expectations: int, real, bool for constants and equations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes; component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE reference errors; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
Component type and implementation structural extraction
